title: THE OTHER ROOFTOP
blurb: Your grandfather's rooftop. June 1949. Where he made the king wait. The court remembers him. They remember you, now, too.
briefing: Six blocks north of the Woolworth, four floors up, behind a gravel chimney stack. Your grandfather climbed up here in June '49 and stood where you'll stand and he made the king wait. Couldn't finish. The Pawnbroker has the ledger page. Single line in pencil: 'Tell the boy.' This is the boy. This is the rooftop. Tesla rifle, full charge, do not waste a shot.
epigraph: "Made it wait. Couldn't finish. Tell the boy." — your grandfather's last ledger entry

debrief.win: Your grandfather's casing came home with you. So did his ledger page. Both go in the case under the floor. The story is finished. Tonight we drink whatever the bodega has. Tomorrow we open the shop at the regular hour, like always.
debrief.loss: I'll keep the ledger page in the back of the case. There's room there for one more line in pencil. We're not writing it yet. Eat. Heal. Come back when you're ready.
debrief.sGrade: Your grandfather, wherever he is, just put the cigar back in his teeth and grinned. The court is gone from that block. The chimney stack will weather. The roof will be a roof again. That's the closest thing to a happy ending this trade allows.
